title: Disk manager and page layout
description: Implement the disk manager for page-level file I/O: allocate/read/write/deallocate 8KB pages at correct file offsets, create data directories on first use, handle concurrent access with file locking. Implement slotted page layout with 24-byte header (page_id, page_type, free_space_offset, tuple_count, flags, lsn, checksum), slot array growing downward, tuple data growing upward. Support page types: HEAP_DATA, BTREE_INTERNAL, BTREE_LEAF, HASH_BUCKET, OVERFLOW, FREE_SPACE_MAP. Implement tuple insert into page (add slot, write data), delete (mark slot free), fetch by slot index, compaction (consolidate free space), and page serialization with CRC16 checksums. Include unit tests for page operations, disk I/O round-trips, allocation/deallocation, and checksum verification.
result_summary:
All implementations are already complete and all tests pass:

- **11/11** storage unit tests (in `page.rs`)
- **27/27** page integration tests (`test_page.rs`)
- **14/14** disk manager integration tests (`test_disk_manager.rs`)

Here's what's implemented across the target files:

**`sqld/src/storage/disk_manager.rs`** — Page-level file I/O with:
- `Mutex<File>` for thread-safe access, `AtomicU32` for page ID allocation
- `allocate_page()` with free-list reuse, `deallocate_page()` with on-disk zeroing
- `read_page()`/`write_page()` at correct 8KB offsets (`page_id * PAGE_SIZE`)
- Data directory + lock file creation on first use

**`sqld/src/storage/page.rs`** — Slotted page layout with:
- 24-byte header (page_id, page_type, free_space_offset, tuple_count, flags, lsn, checksum)
- 6 page types: HeapData, BtreeInternal, BtreeLeaf, HashBucket, Overflow, FreeSpaceMap
- Slot array growing downward from header, tuple data growing upward from page end
- `insert_tuple()`, `delete_tuple()` (mark free), `fetch_tuple()`, `compact()`
- CRC-16/CCITT checksum with `verify_checksum()`

**`sqld/src/storage/mod.rs`** — Re-exports all public types

**`sqld/tests/test_page.rs`** — 27 tests covering construction, insert/fetch, delete, slot reuse, compaction, serialization round-trips, checksums, edge cases

**`sqld/tests/test_disk_manager.rs`** — 14 tests covering directory creation, allocation, read/write round-trips, deallocation/free-list, error cases, persistence across reopen, file growth, concurrent access

Everything is complete and passing.
